zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Lawrence於1557年出生在英國英格蘭格盧斯特斯爾洛德波洛時，她的父親William Lawrence34歲，母親Katherine Neville Beaumont25歲。她於1588年與John Hullings在英國格盧斯特斯爾明音哈普頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和4個女兒的父母。她去世於1621年4月6日在美國麻薩諸塞州享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Lawrence于1557年出生在英国英格兰格盧斯特斯爾洛德波洛时，她的父亲William Lawrence34岁，母亲Katherine Neville Beaumont25岁。她于1588年与John Hullings在英国格盧斯特斯爾明音哈普頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和4个女儿的父母。她去世于1621年4月6日在美国马萨诸塞州享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: Lawrence
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English and Old French personal name Lorens, Laurence, from Latin Laurentius ‘man from Laurentum’, a place in Italy probably named from its laurels or bay trees. The name was borne by a Christian saint who was martyred at Rome in the 3rd century AD ; he enjoyed a considerable cult throughout Europe, with consequent popularity of the personal name (French Laurent, Italian, Spanish Lorenzo, Catalan Llorenç, Portuguese Lourenço, German Laurenz, Polish Wawrzyniec, etc.). In Britain this is a common name from the 12th century, with pet forms such as Law , Low , Lawrie , Laurie , Larry , Larkin , all of which are represented in surnames. There was also a feminine form Laurencia which may have given rise to the English surname. The surname is also borne by Jews among whom it is presumably an Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Ashkenazic surnames. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed many cognates from other languages, e.g. German Lorenz , and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Slovenian Lavrenčič and Lovrenčič (patronymics from Lavrencij and Lovrenc, equivalents of Lawrence), Polish Wawrzyniak . Compare Larrance , Laurence , Lawerence , Lieurance , and Lowrance .

variants.name: Larsson
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Lars, Erik, Anders, Fredrik, Nels, Nils, Olle, Per, Sten, Algot, Birgit, Bjorn.  German Bernhard, Hans, Markus, Oskar.
Swedish: patronymic from the personal name Lars, an equivalent of Lawrence . Compare Larson 1, Larrison , and Lawson 2.
Americanized form of Norwegian and rare North German or Danish patronymic Larssen, a cognate of 1 above. Compare Larsen and Larson 1.

variants.name: Laurance
meanings: English: variant of   Lawrence  .

variants.name: Lafrenz
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Juergen, Arno, Hans, Otto.
German: from a variant of the personal name Laurenz (see Lawrence ).

variants.name: Law
meanings: Scottish and northern English: from a Middle English personal name Law(e), a short form of Lawrence .

Scottish and northern English: topographic name for someone who lived near a hill, northern Middle English law (from Old English hlāw, which generally became low in the south and law in the north). Compare Lowe and Lew .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 羅, based on its Cantonese pronunciation, see   Luo   1.

variants.name: Larsen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Lars, Nels, Niels, Sven, Thor, Knud, Nils, Per, Jorgen, Alf, Bjorn.

Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from the personal name Lars, an equivalent of Lawrence . This is also an Americanized form of the cognate Larssen. Compare Larson 1, Larrison , and Lawson 2.

variants.name: Lassen
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Anders, Erik, Svein, Sven, Thor, Hilmar, Lasse, Niels.  German Hans, Lorenz, Erwin, Manfred, Otto, Uwe.
Danish, Norwegian, and North German: patronymic from Lasse, a pet form of Lars, an equivalent of Lawrence .
North German: possibly also an assimilated form of Danish Larssen (see Larsen ).

variants.name: Larrance
meanings: Altered form of English   Lawrence  .

variants.name: Lakin
meanings: Americanized form of Jewish Leykin (from Belarus), a metronymic from Leyke, a pet form of the Yiddish female personal name Leye, from the Hebrew female personal name Lea, from which English Leah is derived (see Genesis 29: 16) + the Slavic possessive suffix -in.
English: patronymic from the Middle English personal name Lawkin, a diminutive of Law- (a pet form of the personal name Laurence; see Lawrence ). Compare Law 1 and Larkin .

variants.name: Larry
meanings: English: from the personal name Larry, a pet form of Lawrence .

variants.name: Larkin
meanings: English: from a medieval personal name, a diminutive of Lar-, Lor-, both pet forms of Lawrence , formed with the addition of the Middle English suffix -kin.

Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Lorcáin ‘descendant of Lorcán’, a personal name from a diminutive of lorc ‘fierce, cruel’. This Gaelic name was often regarded as an equivalent of English Lawrence .

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Lawrence was born in 1557, in Rodborough, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom, her father, William Lawrence, was 34 and her mother, Katherine Neville Beaumont, was 25. She married John Hullings in 1588, in Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 4 daughters. She died on 6 April 1621, in Massachusetts, United States, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Lawrence est née en 1557, à Rodborough, Gloucestershire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William Lawrence, avait 34 ans et sa mère, Katherine Neville Beaumont, avait 25 ans. Elle a épousé John Hullings en 1588, à Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 4 filles. Elle est décédée le 6 avril 1621, à Massachusetts, États-Unis, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Lawrence 1557 in Rodborough, Gloucestershire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war ihr Vater William Lawrence 34 und ihre Mutter Katherine Neville Beaumont 25. Sie hat 1588 in Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, England John Hullings geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Sie starb am 6. April 1621 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Massachusetts, Vereinigte Staaten.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Lawrence è nata il nel 1557 nel Rodborough, Gloucestershire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, sua padre, William Lawrence, aveva 34 anni e sua madre, Katherine Neville Beaumont, aveva 25 anni. Lei sposato John Hullings nel 1588, nel Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 4 figlie. Morì il 6 aprile 1621, nel Massachusetts, Stati Uniti d'America, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Lawrenceが1557年にイギリスイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアロドボロウで生まれた時，父William Lawrenceは34歳，母Katherine Neville Beaumontは25歳であった。1588年にイギリスグロウセステルシュアイアミンチンハンプトンでJohn Hullingsと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と4人の娘の親であった。彼女は1621年4月6日にアメリカ合衆国マサチューセッツ州で64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Lawrence(이)가 1557년 영국 잉글랜드 그루스스터실르 로드버루그h에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William Lawrence(은)는 34세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Katherine Neville Beaumont(은)는 25세였다. 그녀는 1588년 영국 그루스스터실르 민친함프턴에서 John Hullings(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1621년 4월 6일 미국 매사추세츠 주에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Lawrence nasceu em 1557, em Rodborough, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William Lawrence, tinha 34 anos e sua mãe, Katherine Neville Beaumont, tinha 25 anos. Ela casou-se com John Hullings em 1588, em Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 4 filhas. Ela faleceu em 6 de abril de 1621, em Massachusetts, Estados Unidos da América, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Lawrence родилась в 1557 г. в местности родбороую, глоусестиршир, Англия, Великобритания, её отцу по имени William Lawrence было 34 года, а матери, которую звали Katherine Neville Beaumont, 25. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Hullings в 1588 г. в местности минчинамптон, глоусестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 4 дочери. Она умерла 6 апреля 1621 г. в местности Массачусетс, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Margaret Lawrence nació en 1557, en Rodborough, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William Lawrence, tenía 34 años y su mamá, Katherine Neville Beaumont, tenía 25 años. Se casó con John Hullings en 1588, en Minchinhampton, Gloucestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 4 hijas. Murió el 6 de abril de 1621, en Masachusets, Estados Unidos, a la edad de 64 años.
